official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Construct, write, decode, and observe the clear_titles resetTimes boolean body
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound clear_titles frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns clear_titles immediately after chunks_biomes.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the clear_titles id to ClientboundClearTitlesPacket.
The official clear_titles body is one FriendlyByteBuf boolean named resetTimes.
The resetTimes=true fixture uses the official ClientboundClearTitlesPacket(boolean) constructor, so it does not require initialized client title state, screen, Level, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded resetTimes values, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.

This contract does not prove title UI clearing behavior, title timing reset behavior, initialized client screen/title state, runtime Play entry, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer title UI clearing behavior, title timing reset behavior, initialized client screen/title state, runtime Play entry, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
